name: Gin
what_it_does: High-performance HTTP web framework for Go. Gin provides a radix tree router, middleware chain, request binding/validation, and JSON rendering with minimal overhead. Benchmarks at 40x faster than Martini with comparable API. The most popular Go web framework for building REST APIs and agent backend services. Used widely for microservices and API servers requiring Go's performance.
use_cases: Build high-performance agent API backends in Go with Gin's fast routing and middleware pipeline
Create REST API servers for agent services with automatic request validation and JSON response helpers
Implement agent middleware chains for auth, rate limiting, logging, and request tracing in Go
Build concurrent agent services that handle many simultaneous requests with Go's goroutine model
Deploy lightweight agent microservices as single Go binaries with minimal dependencies
not_for: Python or JavaScript ecosystems — Gin is Go-only; use FastAPI or Express for Python/Node
Full-stack web apps with server-side rendering — Gin is API-focused; use Hugo or Templ for full-stack
Complex ORM requirements — Gin has no built-in ORM; pair with GORM or sqlx
best_when: You're building a high-performance Go REST API or agent backend where you want fast routing, middleware, and request binding without a heavy framework.
avoid_when: You're not using Go, or you need a full-stack framework — use Go's stdlib net/http for ultra-minimal, or Echo/Fiber for similar Go frameworks.

auth.notes: No built-in auth — implement via middleware. Common pattern: gin-jwt middleware or custom Bearer token middleware.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits — runs on your infrastructure

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Binding errors include field-level validation messages. Gin wraps errors in context.Error() chain. Recovery middleware catches panics and returns 500 with error.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: HTTP request handling is stateless — idempotency is application responsibility.
known_agent_gotchas: c.ShouldBindJSON() reads the body once — calling it twice returns EOF; use c.GetRawData() to read and rebind if needed in middleware
Gin's router groups share middleware — middleware added to group applies to all routes in that group including sub-groups; be careful with auth middleware scope
Release mode (gin.SetMode(gin.ReleaseMode)) disables debug logging but also removes route listing — always set in production
Context (gin.Context) is not safe for concurrent access from multiple goroutines — use c.Copy() to pass context to goroutines spawned during request handling
Gin does not use standard library context.Context directly — c.Request.Context() gets the underlying context; use this for deadline propagation to databases
Default 32MB multipart memory limit — increase with r.MaxMultipartMemory for large file uploads in agent file handling endpoints

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS handled at server level. No built-in auth — must implement. Go's type system prevents many injection vulnerabilities. CORS middleware available.
